title: aconitum kiyomiense
intro: Aconitum kiyomiense is a species of flowering plant in the genus Aconitum, commonly known as monk's hood or wolf's bane, which is a perennial plant native to Japan. The plant contains aconitine, a toxic compound with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat pain, inflammation, and fever. In herbal medicine, Aconitum kiyomiense preparations are used to alleviate symptoms of rheumatism, gout, and arthritis, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce stress, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. Herbal preparations of Aconitum kiyomiense include dried roots and rhizomes, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, but caution is advised due to the plant's toxicity and potential side effects.
